title: Building with Web Components in 2025
content:
The case for web components

Web components have matured significantly over the past three years. Browser support is universal, the tooling ecosystem has caught up, and the pattern of building a component once and using it everywhere — regardless of framework — is now a practical reality rather than an aspiration.

Lit as a foundation

Green Core is built on Lit, Google's lightweight library for writing web components. Lit adds a minimal reactive property system and an efficient template renderer on top of the native custom elements standard. The resulting component code is clean, readable, and compiles to small bundles.

Framework interoperability

One of the key design goals for Green Core is that every component should work identically in React, Angular, and plain HTML. We achieve this through auto-generated wrapper components — thin adapters that translate framework conventions (React's synthetic events, Angular's change detection) to native custom element APIs.

Shadow DOM and styling

Shadow DOM encapsulation means component internals are protected from external stylesheets, but it also means consumers can't easily override internal styles. Green components expose a curated set of CSS custom properties for theming, striking a balance between encapsulation and customisability.
